relational-databases.title: Introduction to Relational Databases
relational-databases.definition: Understanding client/server systems, relational database model, tables, relationships, and SQL basics

sql-intro.title: Introduction to SQL
sql-intro.definition: Overview of SQL statements: DML (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) and DDL (CREATE, ALTER, DROP)

mysql-workbench.title: Using MySQL Workbench
mysql-workbench.definition: How to use MySQL Workbench for database development and administration

select-basics.title: SELECT Statement Basics
select-basics.definition: How to retrieve data from a single table using SELECT, column specifications, and aliases

where-clause.title: The WHERE Clause
where-clause.definition: How to filter rows using comparison operators, AND/OR, IN, BETWEEN, LIKE, and IS NULL

logical-operators.title: Logical Operators
logical-operators.definition: Using AND, OR, NOT, IN, BETWEEN to combine filter conditions

order-by.title: The ORDER BY Clause
order-by.definition: Sorting result sets by one or more columns in ascending or descending order

joins-intro.title: Introduction to Joins
joins-intro.definition: Combining data from multiple tables using INNER JOIN, OUTER JOIN, and cross joins

inner-join.title: Inner Joins
inner-join.definition: Retrieving matching rows from two or more tables

outer-join.title: Outer Joins
outer-join.definition: Retrieving all rows from one table and matching rows from another (LEFT, RIGHT, FULL)

insert-statement.title: The INSERT Statement
insert-statement.definition: Adding new rows to a table using INSERT INTO with VALUES or SELECT

update-statement.title: The UPDATE Statement
update-statement.definition: Modifying existing rows in a table with UPDATE and SET clauses

delete-statement.title: The DELETE Statement
delete-statement.definition: Removing rows from a table using DELETE with WHERE clause

aggregate-functions.title: Aggregate Functions
aggregate-functions.definition: Using COUNT, SUM, AVG, MAX, MIN to calculate summary values

having-clause.title: The HAVING Clause
having-clause.definition: Filtering grouped results based on aggregate conditions

subqueries.title: Subqueries
subqueries.definition: Using queries nested inside other queries for complex data retrieval

correlated-subquery.title: Correlated Subqueries
correlated-subquery.definition: Subqueries that reference columns from the outer query

data-types.title: MySQL Data Types
data-types.definition: Understanding numeric, string, date/time, and large object data types

mysql-functions.title: MySQL Functions
mysql-functions.definition: String, numeric, date/time, and conversion functions

database-design.title: Database Design
database-design.definition: How to design databases using ERDs and normalization

normalization.title: Normalization
normalization.definition: Organizing data to reduce redundancy and improve integrity

create-table.title: Creating Tables and Indexes
create-table.definition: Using CREATE TABLE, ALTER TABLE, CREATE INDEX, and DROP statements

views.title: Views
views.definition: Creating and using virtual tables based on SELECT queries

transactions.title: Transactions and Locking
transactions.definition: Ensuring data integrity with ACID transactions and concurrency control

stored-procedures.title: Stored Procedures and Functions
stored-procedures.definition: Creating reusable SQL code blocks stored in the database

triggers.title: Triggers and Events
triggers.definition: Automating SQL execution on data changes and scheduled events

database-security.title: Database Security
database-security.definition: Managing users, privileges, roles, and securing database access

backup-restore.title: Backup and Restore
backup-restore.definition: Strategies and commands for backing up and restoring MySQL databases
